rationale.R1.2: The section makes precise technical claims—R(τ) = P(C ≥ τ) as a survival-function analogue, DG = C_train − C_deploy as an attribution device, and the cliff-threshold as a binary gate—each of which requires checking for internal logical consistency and definitional coherence.
rationale.R1.6: 'Review this section' in an academic writing context centrally implies editorial feedback: sentence clarity, rhetorical flow across the three conceptual moves (reliability function → deployment gap → market-eligibility cliff), and precision of expression.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical/logical soundness (verifying that definitions, formal claims, and the CVaR analogy are internally consistent and well-scoped) or editorial writing quality (sentence clarity, paragraph flow, and prose precision)?
